items.title: Arsenal star David Raya admits Champions League final heartache ‘destroyed me inside’ and ‘couldn’t talk’ to Spain pal
items.summary: DAVID RAYA has opened up on the emotional agony of losing the Champions League final. The Arsenal star revealed the defeat “destroyed me inside” and left him unable to speak to his Spain teammate in the immediate aftermath. Raya reflected on the crushing night after his side fell short on penalties

items.title: Saka trains alone as England prepare for Ghana game
items.summary: Bukayo Saka was the only player not present in group training as England continued their preparations for their second World Cup match against Ghana.

items.title: Thomas Tuchel has cured England’s biggest sticking point that cursed Golden Generation, reveals Michael Owen
items.summary: FOR a generation of England players haunted by near-misses, Michael Owen believes Thomas Tuchel may have already solved the problem that held them back. items.title: Bukayo Saka training away from England squad as Thomas Tuchel’s side face injury niggles ahead of Ghana clash
items.summary: BUKAYO SAKA is training away from the England squad ahead of Tuesday's World Cup group stage clash with Ghana due to an injury concern. items.title: How does qualification for the World Cup knockout stage work?
items.summary: With the second round of group-stage matches under way, we assess who can play who in the knockout rounds - and how they can get there.

items.summary: Mauricio Pochettino had to do without his best player against Australia. items.title: Ex-Prem star out for World Cup revenge after six months unemployed and relegation from third tier
items.summary: WHEN Curacao punched their ticket to the World Cup in November, star striker Jurgen Locadia didn’t even have a club. items.title: Former Argentina World Cup star undergoes emergency heart surgery after collapsing during press conference
items.summary: FORMER Argentina star Fernando Gago has undergone emergency heart surgery. items.title: ITV smashes BBC in World Cup TV ratings war with FOUR of top five audiences and record England views in blow to Beeb
items.summary: ITV is winning the UK battle of the World Cup ratings after the opening week of the tournament. The broadcaster has recorded four of the five highest TV audiences, topped by England's 4-2 win over Croatia on Wednesday.
